import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Load business data from MongoDB
business_key = 'var_functions.query_db:8'
business_data = locals().get(business_key)

if business_data is None:
    print('__RESULT__:')
    print(json.dumps({"error": "Business data not found"}))
else:
    # Create dataframe
    df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
    
    # Check structure and extract business_id number and state
    df_business['business_id_num'] = df_business['business_id'].str.extract('businessid_(\d+)')
    
    # Extract state from description (look for state abbreviations at the end of location)
    state_pattern = r'\b([A-Z]{2})\b'
    df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].str.findall(state_pattern).apply(lambda x: x[-1] if x else None)
    
    # Convert review_count to integer
    df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'])
    
    print(f'Total businesses: {len(df_business)}')
    print(f'States found: {df_business["state"].unique()}')
    print(df_business[['business_id', 'state', 'review_count', 'name']].head())
    
    print('__RESULT__:')
    print(df_business.to_json(orient='records', default_handler=str))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Load business data from MongoDB
business_key = 'var_functions.query_db:8'
business_data = locals().get(business_key)

if business_data is None:
    print('__RESULT__:')
    print(json.dumps({"error": "Business data not found"}))
else:
    # Create dataframe
    df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
    
    # Convert review_count to integer
    df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'])
    
    # Extract state from description - look for pattern: "in City, ST,"
    # The state is a 2-letter abbreviation after a comma and before another comma
    state_pattern = r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*,'
    df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].str.extract(state_pattern)
    
    # Check if we have states now
    states_found = df_business['state'].notna().sum()
    print(f'States extracted: {states_found} out of {len(df_business)} businesses')
    print(df_business[['description', 'state']].head())
    
    # Group by state and sum review counts
    state_reviews = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()
    state_reviews = state_reviews.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)
    
    print('\nTop states by total reviews:')
    print(state_reviews.head())
    
    # Find the state with highest number of reviews
    top_state = state_reviews.iloc[0]
    
    print(f"\nTop state: {top_state['state']} with {top_state['review_count']} reviews")
    
    print('__RESULT__:')
    result = {
        "top_state": top_state['state'],
        "total_reviews": int(top_state['review_count']),
        "state_reviews_ranking": state_reviews.to_dict('records')
    }
    print(json.dumps(result))

import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Load business data from MongoDB
business_key = 'var_functions.query_db:8'
business_data = locals().get(business_key)

if business_data is None:
    print('__RESULT__:')
    print(json.dumps({"error": "Business data not found"}))
else:
    # Create dataframe
    df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
    
    # Convert review_count to integer
    df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'])
    
    # Extract state from description - look for pattern: "in City, ST,"
    # The state is a 2-letter abbreviation after a comma and before another comma
    state_pattern = r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\s*,'
    df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].str.extract(state_pattern)
    
    # Check if we have states now
    states_found = df_business['state'].notna().sum()
    print(f'States extracted: {states_found} out of {len(df_business)} businesses')
    print(df_business[['description', 'state']].head())
    
    # Group by state and sum review counts
    state_reviews = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()
    state_reviews = state_reviews.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)
    
    print('\nTop states by total reviews:')
    print(state_reviews.head())
    
    # Find the state with highest number of reviews
    top_state = state_reviews.iloc[0]
    
    print(f"\nTop state: {top_state['state']} with {top_state['review_count']} reviews")
    
    print('__RESULT__:')
    result = {
        "top_state": top_state['state'],
        "total_reviews": int(top_state['review_count']),
        "state_reviews_ranking": state_reviews.to_dict('records')
    }
    print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Load business data from MongoDB
business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:8')

if not business_data:
    result = {'error': 'Business data not found'}
else:
    df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
    df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'])
    
    # Extract state: Look for pattern like 'City, ST, this' 
    pattern = r'[,\s]+([A-Z]{2})[,\s]+this|the'
    df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].str.extract(pattern)
    
    # Count states found
    states_found = df_business['state'].notna().sum()
    print('States extracted:', states_found, 'of', len(df_business))
    
    # Show first few rows
    print(df_business[['description', 'state']].head(5).to_string())
    
    # Get state with most reviews
    state_grp = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()
    state_grp = state_grp.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)
    
    print('\nTop states:')
    print(state_grp.head())
    
    if not state_grp.empty and state_grp.iloc[0]['state']:
        top = state_grp.iloc[0]
        result = {
            'top_state': top['state'],
            'total_reviews': int(top['review_count']),
            'all_states': state_grp.to_dict('records')
        }
    else:
        result = {'error': 'No state data extracted'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:8')

else:

    df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].str.extract(pattern)
    
    states_found = df_business['state'].notna().sum()
    print('States extracted:', states_found, 'of', len(df_business))
    
    print(df_business[['description', 'state']].head().to_string())
    
    df_state = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()

    if not df_state.empty:
        top = df_state.iloc[0]
        result = {
            'top_state': top['state'],
            'total_reviews': int(top['review_count']),
            'state_ranking': df_state.to_dict('records')
        }
    else:
        result = {'error': 'No state data'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd
import re

business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:8')

if not business_data:
    result = {'error': 'Business data not found'}
else:
    df_business = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
    df_business['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(df_business['review_count'])
    
    # Extract state from text like "Goleta, CA,"
    df_business['state'] = df_business['description'].str.extract(', ([A-Z]{2}),')
    
    count_with_state = df_business['state'].notna().sum()
    print('States found:', count_with_state, 'out of', len(df_business))
    
    # Group by state and sum review counts
    state_totals = df_business.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()
    state_totals = state_totals.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)
    
    # Get top state
    if not state_totals.empty and state_totals.iloc[0]['state']:
        top_state = state_totals.iloc[0]
        result = {
            'state_with_most_reviews': top_state['state'],
            'total_reviews_in_state': int(top_state['review_count']),
            'state_ranking': state_totals.to_dict('records')
        }
    else:
        result = {'error': 'No states extracted from descriptions'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Load reviews data from file path
reviews_path = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:22')
business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:8')

# Read the reviews data from file
with open(reviews_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews_data = json.load(f)

print('Reviews loaded:', len(reviews_data))
print('Businesses loaded:', len(business_data))

# Process business data: extract ID and state
business_df = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
business_df['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(business_df['review_count'])

# Extract business ID number
business_df['biz_id_num'] = business_df['business_id'].str.extract('businessid_(\d+)')

# Extract state from description pattern: ", ST,"
pattern = r', ([A-Z]{2}),'
business_df['state'] = business_df['description'].str.extract(pattern)

# Process reviews: extract business ref number
reviews_df = pd.DataFrame(reviews_data)
reviews_df['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(reviews_df['rating'])
reviews_df['biz_ref_num'] = reviews_df['business_ref'].str.extract('businessref_(\d+)')

print('Businesses with state info:', business_df['state'].notna().sum())
print('Sample businesses:')
print(business_df[['business_id', 'state', 'review_count']].head())

print('\nSample reviews:')
print(reviews_df[['business_ref', 'rating', 'biz_ref_num']].head())

# Group business by state to find top state
state_reviews = business_df[business_df['state'].notna()].groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()
state_reviews = state_reviews.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)

print('\nTop states by total reviews:')
print(state_reviews)

if not state_reviews.empty:
    top_state = state_reviews.iloc[0]['state']
    print('\nTop state:', top_state)
    
    # Get businesses in the top state
    top_state_businesses = business_df[business_df['state'] == top_state]
    print('Businesses in top state:', len(top_state_businesses))
    
    # Get business IDs in top state
    top_state_biz_ids = set(top_state_businesses['biz_id_num'].tolist())
    
    # Filter reviews for businesses in top state
    top_state_review_mask = reviews_df['biz_ref_num'].isin(top_state_biz_ids)
    top_state_reviews = reviews_df[top_state_review_mask]
    
    print('Reviews for top state:', len(top_state_reviews))
    
    if not top_state_reviews.empty:
        avg_rating = top_state_reviews['rating'].mean()
        result = {
            'top_state': top_state,
            'total_reviews': int(state_reviews.iloc[0]['review_count']),
            'review_count_used': len(top_state_reviews),
            'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2)
        }
    else:
        result = {'error': 'No reviews found for top state'}
else:
    result = {'error': 'Could not determine top state'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

val_args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Load reviews data from file path
reviews_path = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:22')
business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:8')

# Read the reviews data from file
with open(reviews_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews_data = json.load(f)

print('Reviews loaded:', len(reviews_data))
print('Businesses loaded:', len(business_data))

# Process business data: extract ID and state
business_df = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
business_df['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(business_df['review_count'])

# Extract business ID number
business_df['biz_id_num'] = business_df['business_id'].str.extract('businessid_(\d+)')

# Extract state from description pattern: ", ST,"
pattern = r', ([A-Z]{2}),'
business_df['state'] = business_df['description'].str.extract(pattern)

# Process reviews: extract business ref number
reviews_df = pd.DataFrame(reviews_data)
reviews_df['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(reviews_df['rating'])
reviews_df['biz_ref_num'] = reviews_df['business_ref'].str.extract('businessref_(\d+)')

print('Businesses with state info:', business_df['state'].notna().sum())
print('Sample businesses:')
print(business_df[['business_id', 'state', 'review_count']].head())

print('\nSample reviews:')
print(reviews_df[['business_ref', 'rating', 'biz_ref_num']].head())

# Group business by state to find top state
state_reviews = business_df[business_df['state'].notna()].groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()
state_reviews = state_reviews.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)

print('\nTop states by total reviews:')
print(state_reviews)

if not state_reviews.empty:
    top_state = state_reviews.iloc[0]['state']
    print('\nTop state:', top_state)
    
    # Get businesses in the top state
    top_state_businesses = business_df[business_df['state'] == top_state]
    print('Businesses in top state:', len(top_state_businesses))
    
    # Get business IDs in top state
    top_state_biz_ids = set(top_state_businesses['biz_id_num'].tolist())
    
    # Filter reviews for businesses in top state
    top_state_review_mask = reviews_df['biz_ref_num'].isin(top_state_biz_ids)
    top_state_reviews = reviews_df[top_state_review_mask]
    
    print('Reviews for top state:', len(top_state_reviews))
    
    if not top_state_reviews.empty:
        avg_rating = top_state_reviews['rating'].mean()
        result = {
            'top_state': top_state,
            'total_reviews': int(state_reviews.iloc[0]['review_count']),
            'review_count_used': len(top_state_reviews),
            'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2)
        }
    else:
        result = {'error': 'No reviews found for top state'}
else:
    result = {'error': 'Could not determine top state'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

args.code:
import json, pandas as pd, re

business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:8')
reviews_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:22')

if not business_data or not reviews_data:
    result = {'error': 'Missing data'}
else:
    # Read reviews from file path
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
    
    business_df = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
    business_df['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(business_df['review_count'])
    
    # Extract state from description
    pattern = r', ([A-Z]{2}),'
    business_df['state'] = business_df['description'].str.extract(pattern)
    
    # Find state with most reviews
    state_counts = business_df.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()
    state_counts = state_counts.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)
    
    if not state_counts.empty and state_counts.iloc[0]['state']:
        top_state = state_counts.iloc[0]['state']
        
        # Get business ID numbers for top state
        top_biz_ids = business_df[business_df['state'] == top_state]['business_id'].str.extract('businessid_(\d+)')[0].tolist()
        
        # Process reviews: extract business ref numbers
        reviews_df = pd.DataFrame(reviews)
        reviews_df['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(reviews_df['rating'])
        reviews_df['biz_ref_num'] = reviews_df['business_ref'].str.extract('businessref_(\d+)')[0]
        
        # Filter reviews for top state
        top_state_reviews = reviews_df[reviews_df['biz_ref_num'].isin(top_biz_ids)]
        
        if not top_state_reviews.empty:
            avg_rating = top_state_reviews['rating'].mean()
            result = {
                'state_with_most_reviews': top_state,
                'total_reviews': int(state_counts.iloc[0]['review_count']),
                'review_count_used': len(top_state_reviews),
                'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2)
            }
        else:
            result = {'error': 'No reviews found for top state'}
    else:
        result = {'error': 'Could not determine top state'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

import json, pandas as pd, re

business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:8')
reviews_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db:22')

if not business_data or not reviews_data:
    result = {'error': 'Missing data'}
else:
    # Read reviews from file path
    with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
    
    business_df = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
    business_df['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(business_df['review_count'])
    
    # Extract state from description
    pattern = r', ([A-Z]{2}),'
    business_df['state'] = business_df['description'].str.extract(pattern)
    
    # Find state with most reviews
    state_counts = business_df.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()
    state_counts = state_counts.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)
    
    if not state_counts.empty and state_counts.iloc[0]['state']:
        top_state = state_counts.iloc[0]['state']
        
        # Get business ID numbers for top state
        top_biz_ids = business_df[business_df['state'] == top_state]['business_id'].str.extract('businessid_(\d+)')[0].tolist()
        
        # Process reviews: extract business ref numbers
        reviews_df = pd.DataFrame(reviews)
        reviews_df['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(reviews_df['rating'])
        reviews_df['biz_ref_num'] = reviews_df['business_ref'].str.extract('businessref_(\d+)')[0]
        
        # Filter reviews for top state
        top_state_reviews = reviews_df[reviews_df['biz_ref_num'].isin(top_biz_ids)]
        
        if not top_state_reviews.empty:
            avg_rating = top_state_reviews['rating'].mean()
            result = {
                'state_with_most_reviews': top_state,
                'total_reviews': int(state_counts.iloc[0]['review_count']),
                'review_count_used': len(top_state_reviews),
                'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2)
            }
        else:
            result = {'error': 'No reviews found for top state'}
    else:
        result = {'error': 'Could not determine top state'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!
